MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: EMILY WECHT, ESQ. for for plaintiff and moving party and specially appearing for Palo Alto Bicycles, Inc., defendant and opposing party.

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED:
PLAINTIFF LEO FOSTER's Motion To Compel A Further Response To Special Interrogatory No. 3 To Defendant Palo Alto Bicycles, Inc. is granted. MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Lawrence Baker, Esq. of Buresh, Kaplan, Jang & Feller, 510-548-7474 for defendant and moving party Palo Alto Bicycles, Inc.

RULING:
THE COURT PASSED THE MATTER AT 9:40AM TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY AND OPPORTUNITY TO APEAR BY COURT CALL. AT 10:10AM THE MATTER WAS RE-CALLED. THE MOVING PARTY DID NOT APEAR. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT MING CYCLE INDUSTRIAL COMPANY, LTD.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS DENIED. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)
